meta.title: F40 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 1 Series F40 engine — from the 1.5 B38 to the 2.0 B48 and B47 diesel. Real UK costs, honest reliability data. Compare prices from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 1 Series F40 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned F40 engine costs between £2,800 and £6,500 supply-only, depending on the engine — the B38 1.5 petrol ranges £2,800–£4,500, the B48 2.0 petrol runs £3,200–£6,500, and the B47 diesel runs £2,800–£5,000. Fitting adds 8–16 hours of labour at £65–£90/hr. Use our calculator for a personalised quote.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 1 Series F40 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B38 1.5-litre three-cylinder petrol (116i/118i) is the most reliable engine in the F40 line-up — it's the simplest architecture, with no complex cooling system issues and a well-documented reliability record. The B48 four-cylinder petrol is also highly reliable, with only minor cooling system concerns.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 1 Series F40?

acceptedAnswer.text: EGR cooler failure on the B47 diesel (pre-recall units), cooling system leaks (water pump, expansion tank) on the B38 and B48 petrol engines, and DPF clogging on diesels used predominantly for urban driving. BMW issued a recall for the B47 EGR cooler — check your car's recall status immediately.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 1 Series F40 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the F40's modular engines (B38, B48, B47) are significantly more reliable than the N47/N57 engines they replaced. The B47 diesel EGR cooler issue is the main concern, but it's recall-addressed. Petrol engines are generally very reliable, with only minor cooling system maintenance required.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 1 Series F40 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: Early (2019–2020) B47 diesel units without documented EGR cooler recall work carry the highest risk. Also avoid any diesel used predominantly for short urban journeys without regular DPF regeneration. If you must have a diesel, choose a 2021+ model with documented recall completion.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my F40 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant loss or overheating (petrol or diesel EGR cooler failure), DPF warning lights or reduced power (diesel urban use), or blue smoke and rough idle on any high-mileage unit are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic tool for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 1 Series F40 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% BMW scrap threshold. Against a well-kept £18,000 F40, a £3,800 M135i recon is 21% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £12,000 early example, the same recon is 32% — still safe. For sub-£8,000 examples, evaluate carefully.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW 1 Series F40 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B38 and B48 petrol engines can comfortably exceed 150,000 miles with routine maintenance. The B47 diesel is equally durable, provided the EGR cooler recall work is completed and the car is driven to allow DPF regeneration (regular motorway runs). All three are significantly more durable than the N-series engines they replaced.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the F40 and previous 1 Series generations?

acceptedAnswer.text: The F40 (2019–present) is the third-generation 1 Series and the first to switch from rear-wheel drive to front-wheel drive, based on BMW's FAAR platform shared with the Mini. The E81/E87 (2004–2011) and F20/F21 (2011–2019) were rear-wheel drive. The F40 is also the first 1 Series offered exclusively with three- and four-cylinder engines — no six-cylinder options are available.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 1 Series F40 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate, vehicle documentation, and on a sticker under the bonnet. For 1.5 petrol, look for B38A15 (116i, 118i, 225xe PHEV). For 2.0 petrol, look for B48A20 (120i, 128ti, M135i xDrive). For 2.0 diesel, look for B47D20A (116d, 118d, 120d, 125d). Tell us your variant and year, and we'll confirm.

hero.h1: BMW 1 Series F40 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the F40-generation BMW 1 Series — from the 1.5-litre three-cylinder petrol to the 2.0-litre four-cylinder and the range-topping M135i xDrive. Real UK costs, honest failure data, and the switch to front-wheel drive that redefined this generation. Backed by 344+ real UK 1 Series enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 1 Series F40 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the F40 1 Series specifically — BMW's first front-wheel-drive 1 Series, powered exclusively by three- and four-cylinder modular engines.

overview.intro: The BMW 1 Series F40 is the third generation of BMW's entry-level premium hatchback, launched in July 2019. It marked a significant departure from its predecessors (E81/E87 and F20/F21) by switching from rear-wheel drive to front-wheel drive, based on BMW's FAAR platform shared with the Mini and 2 Series Gran Coupé. The F40 is powered exclusively by BMW's modular three- and four-cylinder engines — the B38 1.5-litre petrol three, B48 2.0-litre petrol four, and B47 2.0-litre diesel four — with power outputs ranging from 109 bhp in the 116i to 306 bhp in the M135i xDrive. A plug-in hybrid variant, the 225xe, combines the B38 petrol with an electric motor for 218 bhp combined output. A 2024 facelift (LCI) introduced revised styling and updated infotainment.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: FAAR (front-wheel-drive architecture, shared with Mini) • Predecessor: BMW 1 Series F20/F21 (2011–2019) • Successor: Current model — on sale • Sibling body styles: None — F40 is sole 1 Series hatchback body style
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The F40 1 Series generated real UK engine enquiries in 2025, with the 2.0-litre B47 diesel and B48 petrol engines accounting for the largest share of BMW 1 Series enquiries (344 total).

items.engine: B47D20A 2.0-litre (190 bhp) 120d — The 190 bhp B47 diesel delivers excellent real-world performance and economy. Post-recall EGR cooler units are reliable, and the engine is significantly more robust than the N47 it replaced.
items.whoItsFor: high-mileage buyers who prioritise economy and torque.

items.engine: B48A20 2.0-litre (178 bhp) 120i — The 178 bhp B48 petrol offers the perfect balance of performance, economy, and reliability for the F40. No supercharger complexity, no twin-turbo issues — just a well-sorted, modern four-cylinder.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the best all-round F40 petrol.

items.engine: B47D20A (pre-EGR recall) — Early B47 diesels (2019–2020) suffer from EGR cooler failure, which can lead to coolant leaks and, in rare cases, fire risk. BMW issued a recall — any car without documented recall work carries this risk.
items.whoItsFor: only buyers with documented EGR cooler replacement.

items.engine: B48A20 (M135i xDrive) — The 306 bhp B48 in the M135i xDrive is the most expensive engine in the F40 line-up to replace, with reconditioned units costing £3,800–£6,500. Cooling system issues (water pump, expansion tank) are the primary risk.
items.whoItsFor: performance buyers who accept the premium.

items.engine: B48A20 2.0-litre (178 bhp) 120i — The 120i offers the best balance of performance, economy, and running costs. The B48 is well-supported, parts are widely available, and the recon cost is significantly lower than the M135i.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want genuine performance without the M135i's premium.

items.engine: B38A15 1.5-litre (136 bhp) 118i — Often dismissed as the "entry-level" F40, the 136 bhp three-cylinder delivers surprising character and genuinely good fuel economy. It's the cheapest F40 to run and the most reliable engine in the range.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who prioritise running costs over outright performance.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Risk vs Reward — check EGR recall status

economicsRule.title: The F40 rule
economicsRule.text: A £3,800–£6,500 M135i recon against a £12,000–£15,000 early F40 sits at 25–54% of the car's value — on a sub-£12,000 example, that brushes our 70% BMW threshold, and we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £18,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 21–36% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F40 Costs Least To Own?
Which F40 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: B48 M135i xDrive — cooling system repairs and xDrive complexity add cost.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F40 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: B48 2.0-litre 120i — the sweet spot between performance and running costs.

problems.title: B47 Diesel EGR Cooler Failure
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler on early B47 diesel engines can develop internal coolant leaks, leading to coolant loss and, in rare cases, fire risk. BMW issued a recall for this issue — any car without documented recall work carries this risk.
problems.recommendation: On any F40 diesel, check the EGR cooler recall status immediately — a £500–£1,200 preventative repair is cheaper than the £2,800–£5,000 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare B47D20A engine prices for your 1 Series →

problems.title: B48/B38 Cooling System Leaks
problems.rootCause: The cooling system on B38 and B48 engines — including the expansion tank, water pump, and plastic fittings — can develop leaks over time. Coolant loss can lead to overheating and, if ignored, engine damage.

tieredCosts.work: Water pump, expansion tank, coolant pipes

problems.recommendation: On any F40 petrol approaching 60,000 miles, have the cooling system inspected — a £300–£800 preventative repair is cheaper than the £2,800–£6,500 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare B48/B38 engine prices for your 1 Series →

problems.title: B47 Diesel DPF Clogging (Urban Use)
problems.rootCause: Diesel particulate filters clog with soot when the car is used predominantly for short urban journeys that don't allow the DPF to regenerate. Warning lights and reduced power are the symptoms; ignored, it can require DPF replacement or engine damage.

tieredCosts.work: DPF replacement or full engine replacement

problems.recommendation: On any F40 diesel used predominantly in urban environments, ensure regular motorway runs to allow DPF regeneration — a £300–£800 preventative service is cheaper than the £800–£1,500 DPF replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare B47D20A engine prices for your 1 Series →

replacementCosts.h2: F40 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are F40 1 Series only. The F40 marked BMW's switch to front-wheel drive — the M135i xDrive uses BMW's xDrive all-wheel-drive system to deliver 306 bhp. The 128ti is a front-wheel-drive hot hatch positioned between the 120i and M135i. The 225xe is the plug-in hybrid variant.
